terms.definition: A document that defines the terms and conditions under which a contingent worker is engaged by a buyer through a supplier.
A formal document that captures and defines the work activities, deliverables, and timeline that a vendor must execute in performance of specified work for a client.
An external worker (contractor, consultant, temporary worker) who is not a full-time employee of the buyer organization.
The organization procuring contingent workforce or services through SAP Fieldglass.
The staffing firm or service provider that supplies workers or services to the buyer through SAP Fieldglass.
Software that manages the procurement and management of contingent labor and services, including SAP Fieldglass.
A record of hours worked by a contingent worker during a given period, submitted for approval and payment.
The sequence of approval steps required before a work item (work order, time sheet, invoice) is finalized in SAP Fieldglass.
An identifier for a specific SAP Fieldglass business module (e.g., work orders, statements of work) used in API routing.
A business document in SAP Fieldglass that requires an approval action, such as a work order, time sheet, or invoice.
The authorization protocol used by SAP Fieldglass APIs to authenticate and authorize API clients.
A configurable list of values in SAP Fieldglass used for field selection in forms and integration, such as worker types or cost centers.
A chronological record of changes and actions performed in SAP Fieldglass for compliance and traceability.
A verification process for contingent workers integrated with SAP Fieldglass to confirm worker eligibility.
System for Cross-domain Identity Management — a standard protocol used by the SAP Fieldglass Identity Management API for user provisioning.
Open Data Protocol used by the SAP Fieldglass analytic API to provide standardized access to business analytics data.
Reporting and analytics capabilities in SAP Fieldglass providing insights into workforce spend, headcount, and performance.
